zh.Stoke by Nayland, Suffolk, England: 英國素福爾可斯托克 白 納易藍德
zh.lifeSummary: Edward Braddrock Goodwyn於1470年出生在英國。他於1489年與Elizabeth Brown在英國英格蘭結婚。他們至少是1個女兒的父母。

zh-hans.Stoke by Nayland, Suffolk, England: 英国素福爾可斯托克 白 納易藍德
zh-hans.lifeSummary: Edward Braddrock Goodwyn于1470年出生在英国。他于1489年与Elizabeth Brown在英国英格兰结婚。他们至少是1个女儿的父母。

nameMeanings.name: Goodwyn
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (East Anglia and Middlesex): variant of   Goodwin  .

variants.name: Goodwin
meanings: English: from the Middle English personal name Godwin (Old English Gōdwine). Compare Godwin .
Irish: adopted in Mayo for Ó Goidín, a variant of Ó Godáin, ‘descendant of Godán’, a diminutive personal name from got ‘stammering, lisping’.

History: This name was brought independently to New England by many bearers from the 17th century onward. William Goodwin was one of the founders of Hartford, CT, in 1635 (coming from Cambridge, MA with Thomas Hooker).

nameMeanings.name: Edward
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old English personal name derived from ēad ‘prosperity, riches’ + weard ‘guard’. This has been one of the most successful of all Old English names, in frequent use from before the Conquest to the present day, and even being exported into other European languages. It was the name of three Anglo-Saxon kings and has been borne by eight kings of England since the Norman Conquest. It is also the name of the youngest son of Queen Elizabeth II . The most influential early bearer was King Edward the Confessor ( ?1002–66 ; ruled 1042–66 ). In a troubled period of English history, he contrived to rule fairly and (for a time at any rate) firmly. But in the latter part of his reign he paid more attention to his religion than to his kingdom. He died childless, and his death sparked off conflicting claims to his throne, which were resolved by the victory of William the Conqueror at the Battle of Hastings. His memory was honoured by Normans and English alike, for his fairness and his piety. Edward's mother was Norman; he had spent part of his youth in Normandy; and William claimed to have been nominated by Edward as his successor. Edward was canonized in the 12th century, and came to be venerated throughout Europe as a model of a Christian king.

nameMeanings.name: Braddrock
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: topographic name for someone living by a notable broad oak, from Old English brād ‘broad’ + āc ‘oak’, or a habitational name from a minor place so named, such as Broad Oak in Symondsbury (Dorset), Broadoak in Leigh and Broadoak in Kingsley (both Staffordshire), a lost Broodok in Macclesfield Hundred (Cheshire), and Broak Oak in Parr (Lancashire).

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

en.lifeSummary: Edward Braddrock Goodwyn was born in 1470, in England. He married Elizabeth Brown in 1489, in England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 daughter.

fr.lifeSummary: Edward Braddrock Goodwyn est né en 1470, à Angleterre. Il a épousé Elizabeth Brown en 1489, à Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fille.

de.lifeSummary: Edward Braddrock Goodwyn wurde 1470 in England geboren. Er hat 1489 in England, United Kingdom Elizabeth Brown geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Tochter.

it.lifeSummary: Edward Braddrock Goodwyn è nato il nel 1470 nel England. Lui sposato Elizabeth Brown nel 1489, nel Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlia.

ja.Stoke by Nayland, Suffolk, England: イギリススフォルクストク ビー ナイランド
ja.lifeSummary: Edward Braddrock Goodwynは1470年にイギリスで生まれた。1489年にイギリスイギリスでElizabeth Brownと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の娘の親であった。

ko.lifeSummary: Edward Braddrock Goodwyn(은)는 1470년 영국에서 태어났다. 그는 1489년 영국 잉글랜드에서 Elizabeth Brown(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다.

pt.lifeSummary: Edward Braddrock Goodwyn nasceu em 1470, em Inglaterra. Ele casou-se com Elizabeth Brown em 1489, em Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filha.

ru.lifeSummary: Edward Braddrock Goodwyn родился в 1470 г. в местности Англия. Он женился на женщине по имени Elizabeth Brown в 1489 г. в местности Англия, Великобритания. У них была по крайней мере 1 дочь.

es.lifeSummary: Edward Braddrock Goodwyn nació en 1470, en Inglaterra. Se casó con Elizabeth Brown en 1489, en Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hija.
